Experiment 1 — Utility–privacy gains under non-IID with DDG-TREE + residualization + DP automation

Objective and hypotheses
- H1: PACER-FL achieves 2–5 point higher accuracy vs DP-FedAvg with i.i.d. Gaussian and vs server-side DP-FTRL at ε in {1,2,4} under non-IID data.
- H2: Residualization shrinks sensitivity: mean residual norm substantially below full gradient norm; clipping utilization 10–20%.
- H3: Aggregate noise growth follows √log T (tree) versus √T (i.i.d.), visible in the slope of log RMS noise vs log T.

Task, data, and model
- Dataset: CIFAR-10 with standard train/test splits; transforms: random crop + flip for train; normalization.
- Non-IID: Dirichlet α=0.1 across N=10,000 clients, minimum 20 samples/client.
- Model: SmallCIFAR10CNN (~1.3M params), as above.

Federated setup
- Rounds T=600, fixed-size cohort per round n_t=200 (sampling fraction q=0.02).
- Local epochs=1, batch size=64, client optimizer SGD(lr=0.05, momentum=0.9).
- Server optimizer: FedAvg with server momentum 0.9; optionally Nesterov off.

Privacy targets and allocation
- δ=1e-6, ε ∈ {1.0, 2.0, 4.0} at T.
- Budget split (guideline): ε_tree ≈ 0.6ε, ε_gate ≈ 0.2ε, ε_clip ≈ 0.2ε. Convert to per-round allocations based on T and restart schedule (NoTreeRestart by default). Gate and clip use pure-epsilon composition (Laplace and DP histogram). Tree noise accounted via RDP with effective noise multipliers per round.

Compared methods
- Non-DP FedAvg (upper bound; no clipping, no noise).
- Baseline A: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian at server; per-layer clipping fixed or tuned DP-quantile (best of both).
- Baseline B: Server-side DP-FTRL (tree-correlated Gaussian), no compression alignment, non-auditable.
- PACER-FL full: DDG-TREE on clients (auditable), residualized DP updates with DIANA-style control variates, DP AboveThreshold gating, DP quantile clipping, FO-aware CountSketch compression.
- Ablations: remove one module at a time: residualization, gating, FO-aware compression, DP quantiles.

Core implementation details
- Residualization/control variates
  • Server maintains c_t (EMA of previous aggregates): c_t = (1−β)c_{t−1} + β ḡ_{t−1}, β≈0.1–0.2.
  • Client i tracks h_i^t, updated with c_t: h_i^{t+1} = (1−α) h_i^t + α c_t, α=0.2.
  • Client computes local gradient/update g_i^t and residual r_i^t = g_i^t − h_i^t.

- Per-layer DP quantile clipping
  • Each client computes per-layer residual L2 norms; contributes to DP histogram using diffprivlib per-round with ε_clip_round.
  • Server sets C_res,t[layer] to the 90th percentile from the DP histogram; clients clip residuals per layer to these thresholds next round.

- DP gating (AboveThreshold)
  • Score s_i^t = max{cos(r_i^t, c_t), λ||r_i^t||} with λ=0.1/C_res,t_avg.
  • Gate decision: ON if Laplace-noised s_i^t ≥ θ_t; else OFF. θ_t is a public threshold schedule aiming for early ON≈0.6, mid≈0.5, late≈0.4 while ensuring min per-user ON≥0.2 via DP-throttled adjustments.
  • OFF clients still upload zero sketch + DDG-TREE shares, preserving indistinguishability under SecAgg.

- FO-aware CountSketch compression
  • Sketch dimension m_t chosen to align compression MSE to DP noise MSE: m_t ≈ κ · min{d, (n_t ε_eff,t M_t)^2}, κ=1.2.
  • M_t estimated as ||μ̂_res,t−1|| / C̄_res,t−1 from the previous round aggregate (no extra privacy cost, as it is a function of the released aggregate).

- DDG-TREE noise
  • Binary tree of height ⌈log2 T⌉; NoTreeRestart initially.
  • Server publishes per-node seeds and target variance in sketch space (target_var_per_node[node]).
  • Clients add per-node DDG discrete Gaussian shares directly in sketch space.
  • L_min calibration: variance is calibrated to be ≥ target for any realized cohort L ≥ L_min; if L < L_min, server adds pre-committed rescue noise (auditable).

- Accounting
  • Per round: add an effective Gaussian event with multiplier σ_eff_t to the Accountant for the aggregate under fixed-size sampling (approximated as Poisson q=0.02 for RDP). Also add pure-eps events for gating and clipping.
  • Numerically adjust σ_eff_t schedule so that total ε at round T matches target (bisection on a global scale factor).

End-to-end training loop skeleton

import torch, numpy as np
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from tqdm import trange

# Helpers to flatten/unflatten model params

def flatten_params(model):
    return torch.cat([p.detach().reshape(-1) for p in model.parameters()])

def assign_params(model, vec):
    offset = 0
    for p in model.parameters():
        num = p.numel()
        p.data.copy_(vec[offset:offset+num].reshape(p.shape))
        offset += num

# Client local step returning residual r_i

def client_compute_residual(model, data_loader, device, h_i_vec, c_t_vec, lr=0.05, momentum=0.9):
    local_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    local_model.load_state_dict(model.state_dict())
    opt = torch.optim.SGD(local_model.parameters(), lr=lr, momentum=momentum)
    local_model.train()
    for xb, yb in data_loader:
        xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        opt.zero_grad()
        logits = local_model(xb)
        loss = torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits, yb)
        loss.backward()
        opt.step()
        break  # 1 epoch with batch_size as provided (simulate partial epoch if needed)
    g_i_vec = (flatten_params(local_model) - flatten_params(model)).detach().cpu().numpy()
    r_i = g_i_vec - h_i_vec
    # Also return per-layer norms if needed for DP hist
    return r_i

# Main server loop

def run_pacer_fl(exp_cfg):
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    # Data
    transform_train = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=4), transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    transform_test = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    trainset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=exp_cfg["data_root"], train=True, download=True, transform=transform_train)
    testset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=exp_cfg["data_root"], train=False, download=True, transform=transform_test)
    labels = np.array(trainset.targets)
    client_indices = dirichlet_noniid_split(labels, num_clients=exp_cfg["N"], alpha=exp_cfg["alpha_dir"], seed=exp_cfg["seed"]) 

    # Server state
    global_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    d = sum(p.numel() for p in global_model.parameters())
    c_t_vec = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
    beta = exp_cfg.get("beta", 0.1)
    alpha_cv = exp_cfg.get("alpha_cv", 0.2)
    accountant = Accountant(delta=exp_cfg["delta"]) 
    L_min = exp_cfg.get("L_min", int(exp_cfg["n_t"]*0.8))

    # Clipping and gating configs
    eps_total = exp_cfg["eps"]
    eps_tree = 0.6 * eps_total
    eps_gate_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_clip_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_gate_round = eps_gate_total / exp_cfg["T"]
    eps_clip_round = eps_clip_total / exp_cfg["T"]

    # Tree seeds and target variances per node (initialized and then scaled)
    # Start with unit variances per node in sketch space; scale globally later to hit eps_tree
    target_var_per_node = {}
    vrf_seeds = {}
    max_depth = int(np.ceil(np.log2(exp_cfg["T"]+1)))
    rng = np.random.default_rng(exp_cfg["seed"]) 
    for t in range(1, exp_cfg["T"]+1):
        for node in tree_nodes_for_round(t):
            if node not in vrf_seeds:
                vrf_seeds[node] = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
            if node not in target_var_per_node:
                target_var_per_node[node] = 1.0  # provisional; scale later

    # Initial sketch dim and sketched noise calibration
    n_t = exp_cfg["n_t"]; q = n_t / exp_cfg["N"]
    eps_eff_round = eps_tree / exp_cfg["T"]
    # Back-of-envelope: set global noise multiplier to meet eps_tree via RDP for Gaussian; calibrate scale
    # We search a scalar s such that composed epsilon ≈ eps_tree.
    def epsilon_for_scale(s):
        acc = Accountant(delta=exp_cfg["delta"])
        for _ in range(exp_cfg["T"]):
            acc.add_gaussian_event(noise_multiplier=s, sampling_probability=q)
        return acc.get_epsilon()
    lo, hi = 0.1, 50.0
    for _ in range(30):
        mid = (lo+hi)/2
        if epsilon_for_scale(mid) > eps_tree: lo = mid
        else: hi = mid
    sigma_eff_global = hi

    # FO-aware m_t control: initialize
    M_prev = 0.1

    # Per-client control variates
    h_i = [np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32) for _ in range(exp_cfg["N"])]

    # Training
    for t in trange(1, exp_cfg["T"]+1):
        # Cohort sampling (fixed size)
        cohort = rng.choice(exp_cfg["N"], size=n_t, replace=False)
        # Set sketch dim m_t
        m_t = choose_m_t(d=d, n_t=n_t, eps_eff_t=eps_eff_round, M_t=M_prev, kappa=1.2)
        sketch_seed = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
        sketch = CountSketch(d=d, m=m_t, seed=sketch_seed)
        # Per-node target variance scaled for sigma_eff_global; distribute equally across nodes
        node_list = list(set([node for round_idx in range(1, t+1) for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_idx)]))
        scale_factor = (sigma_eff_global**2) / max(1e-6, np.mean(list(target_var_per_node.values())))
        target_var_scaled = {k: v*scale_factor for k, v in target_var_per_node.items()}

        # DP quantile clipping: collect per-layer norms via DP histogram
        # For simplicity, we use a single global threshold here; layer-wise requires mapping params to layers and repeating histogram per layer
        per_client_norms = []
        # Clients compute residuals and propose gating scores
        uploads = []  # each is dict with 'sketch', 'shares'
        ON_flags = []
        for i in cohort:
            idxs = client_indices[i]
            loader = DataLoader(Subset(trainset, idxs), batch_size=exp_cfg["batch_size"], shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model, loader, device, h_i[i], c_t_vec)
            per_client_norms.append(np.linalg.norm(r_i))
        # DP histogram to set clipping threshold (P90)
        C_res_t = private_quantile_from_histogram(np.array(per_client_norms), q=0.9, clip_max=float(np.percentile(per_client_norms, 99.9)+1.0), eps=eps_clip_round, rng=rng)

        # Gate threshold schedule (public); aim for ON rate ~0.5 here; simple heuristic using previous norms
        theta_t = 0.0  # threshold on the combined score; tune schedule per phase

        # Client uploads (with clipping, sketch, DDG shares, DP gate)
        realized_ON = 0
        for i, i_client in enumerate(cohort):
            idxs = client_indices[i_client]
            loader = DataLoader(Subset(trainset, idxs), batch_size=exp_cfg["batch_size"], shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model, loader, device, h_i[i_client], c_t_vec)
            # Clip
            norm = np.linalg.norm(r_i) + 1e-12
            scale = min(1.0, C_res_t / norm)
            r_i = (r_i * scale).astype(np.float32)
            # Score and gate
            cos_sim = float(np.dot(r_i, c_t_vec) / (np.linalg.norm(r_i)*np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)+1e-12)) if np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)>0 else 0.0
            s_i = max(cos_sim, 0.1 * np.linalg.norm(r_i) / max(C_res_t, 1e-6))
            ON = dp_gate(s_i, theta_t, sensitivity=1.0, eps=eps_gate_round, rng=rng)
            ON_flags.append(ON)
            realized_ON += int(ON)
            # Sketch
            y_i = sketch.S(r_i)
            # DDG-TREE shares in sketch domain
            shares_i = client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t=t, sketch_dim=m_t, seeds=vrf_seeds, target_var_per_node=target_var_scaled, L_min=L_min, cohort_size=n_t)
            if not ON:
                # Zero out sketch payload but keep DDG shares
                y_i = np.zeros_like(y_i)
            uploads.append({"y": y_i.astype(np.float32), "shares": shares_i})
            # Update client control variate h_i with server c_t
            h_i[i_client] = (1-alpha_cv)*h_i[i_client] + alpha_cv * c_t_vec

        # SecAgg sum of sketches + DDG shares (server sees only sums)
        Y_sum = np.sum([u["y"] for u in uploads], axis=0)
        Shares_sum = np.sum([u["shares"] for u in uploads], axis=0)
        sketch_agg = Y_sum + Shares_sum.astype(np.float32)
        # Unsketch to model space
        agg_update = sketch.unS(sketch_agg)

        # Server momentum + model update
        vec = flatten_params(global_model).cpu().numpy()
        vec_new = vec + agg_update / max(realized_ON, 1)  # normalization by ON clients
        assign_params(global_model, torch.tensor(vec_new, dtype=torch.float32))

        # Update c_t
        c_t_vec = (1-beta) * c_t_vec + beta * (agg_update / max(realized_ON, 1))

        # Accountant update (effective Gaussian + pure eps for gate/clip)
        accountant.add_gaussian_event(noise_multiplier=sigma_eff_global, sampling_probability=q)
        accountant.add_laplace_pure_eps(eps_gate_round)
        accountant.add_laplace_pure_eps(eps_clip_round)

        # Log to ledger
        write_ledger_record(exp_cfg["ledger_path"], {
            "round": t, "vrf_seeds": {str(k): int(v) for k, v in vrf_seeds.items()},
            "target_var_per_node": {str(k): float(v*1.0) for k, v in target_var_scaled.items()},
            "L_min": int(L_min), "realized_cohort": int(n_t),
            "sketch_seed": int(sketch_seed), "sketch_m": int(m_t),
            "clip_quantiles": {"global": float(C_res_t)},
            "gate_theta": float(theta_t),
            "eps_eff_round": float(eps_eff_round),
            "eps_gate_round": float(eps_gate_round),
            "eps_clip_round": float(eps_clip_round),
            "restart_mode": "NoTreeRestart"
        })

        # Evaluate periodically and compute SNR/residual shrinkage statistics, save to metrics log (omitted for brevity)

    final_eps = accountant.get_epsilon()
    print("Total epsilon:", final_eps)
    return global_model

Primary metrics and analysis
- Utility: test accuracy vs rounds and final accuracy at matched ε; average across 3 seeds; report mean ± 95% CI.
- Residual shrinkage: E||r_i|| / E||g_i|| as a function of t (approximate ||g_i|| via initial rounds or local gradient norms without residualization).
- Clipping utilization: fraction of clients with norm > C_res,t.
- SNR per round: ||μ̂_res,t|| / sqrt(Var(noise along μ̂_res,t)) estimated from ledger and reconstructed noise covariance via auditor.
- Noise growth: compute RMS of reconstructed noise across rounds; fit slope of log RMS vs log T; expect ≈ 0.5× slope for √log T versus √T.

Reliability knobs
- 3 independent seeds per condition; report CI via t-intervals.
- Fixed-size cohort draws with enforced size; independent RNG streams for cohort, DDG, sketches.
- Sanity checks: compare PACER with ablations to isolate which module contributes to gains; ensure accountant ε never exceeds target.

Experiment 2 — Communication–efficiency frontier at fixed DP

Objective and hypotheses
- Demonstrate 20–50% uplink reduction for PACER-FL at ε=2, δ=1e-6 with no accuracy loss by aligning sketch error to DP noise floor and privately gating low-value clients.

Data/model and setup
- EMNIST-Balanced, CNN (~1.0M params). N=5,000 clients; α_dir=0.1; rounds T=500; cohort n_t=100; same local training settings as Exp.1.
- Privacy: ε=2 at horizon, δ=1e-6. Budget split as in Exp.1.

Methods
- Baseline C: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian + QSGD-8bit quantization.
- Baseline D: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian + Top-k sparsification (k=1%) with error feedback (use torch.Tensor.topk and EF from Deep Gradient Compression implementations).
- Baseline E: Private FO with Adapt Norm (independent Gaussian assumption) + its autotuned compression.
- PACER-FL full and PACER-wo-gate.

Implementation specifics
- Communication counting
  • CountSketch: m_t 32-bit floats + 64-bit sketch seed id + ~256-bit SecAgg overhead.
  • QSGD-8bit: 8 bits per value + per-layer scale overhead; include seed/indices if needed.
  • Top-k: transmit k indices (log2 d bits each) + k values (16 bits) + EF checksum; we count the exact bits using numpy.ceil(np.log2(d)).

- FO alignment check: compute ratio of empirical sketch MSE to DP noise MSE per round; maintain in [0.7, 1.3].

Selected code snippets

# Estimated DP noise MSE vs sketch MSE

def estimate_dp_noise_mse(sigma_eff, d):
    return d * (sigma_eff**2)

def estimate_sketch_mse(x, sketch: CountSketch):
    y = sketch.S(x)
    x_hat = sketch.unS(y)
    return float(np.mean((x_hat - x)**2))

# QSGD-8bit placeholder using PyTorch quantize_per_tensor

def qsgd8_quantize(x: torch.Tensor):
    scale = x.abs().max() / 127.0 + 1e-12
    q = torch.clamp(torch.round(x / scale), -127, 127).to(torch.int8)
    return q, float(scale)

# Top-k sparsification with EF state

def topk_sparsify(x: torch.Tensor, k_frac=0.01, ef_state=None):
    d = x.numel(); k = max(1, int(d * k_frac))
    if ef_state is None: ef_state = torch.zeros_like(x)
    x_ef = x + ef_state
    vals, idx = torch.topk(x_ef.abs().flatten(), k)
    mask = torch.zeros_like(x_ef.flatten(), dtype=torch.bool)
    mask[idx] = True
    sgn = torch.sign(x_ef.flatten()[mask])
    sparse_vals = x_ef.flatten()[mask]
    # Update EF
    x_hat = torch.zeros_like(x_ef.flatten())
    x_hat[mask] = sparse_vals
    ef_state = (x_ef.flatten() - x_hat).reshape_as(x)
    return sparse_vals, idx, ef_state

Metrics
- Uplink bits per client/round and total; wall-clock proxy (bytes processed/sec) per round.
- Accuracy at horizon and AUC of accuracy vs bits curve across rounds.
- Gating: OFF rate, ON-rate fairness across strata (e.g., by dominant class per client or device tier).
- Alignment ratio: sketch MSE / DP noise MSE target in [0.7, 1.3].

Success criteria
- PACER-FL ≥ 25% less uplink than the best baseline at similar accuracy and privacy.
- OFF/ON indistinguishability: server sees only aggregate sketches; we confirm with a classifier that attempts to infer ON-rate from aggregates performs at chance.

Reliability
- Same 3-seed protocol; ensure same accountant achieves ε=2 for all methods; for baselines, calibrate Gaussian noise to match ε using dp_accounting; equalize clipping thresholds.

Experiment 3 — Auditability and robustness (dropouts, L_min, restarts)

Objective and hypotheses
- Validate that client-synthesized DDG-TREE noise meets/exceeds target covariance under dropouts and varying cohorts; confirm robust accuracy under restart regimes (NoTreeRestart, SometimesRestart, TreeRestart); fairness of DP gating with min-ON constraints.

Setup
- CIFAR-10, ε=2, δ=1e-6; same as Exp.1 otherwise.
- Regimes
  • R1 Mild dropouts: p=0.2; cohort n_t maintained (server resamples until size met).
  • R2 Heavy dropouts: p=0.6; realized cohort may drop below L_min in ~10% rounds; engage rescue noise per ledger.
  • R3 Bursty participation: alternating phases p in {0.2, 0.8}; use SometimesRestart schedule synchronized to phase boundaries.

Tree restart schedules
- NoTreeRestart: nodes persist.
- SometimesRestart: restart at known checkpoints (e.g., powers of 2 or phase boundaries) to adapt noise allocation.
- TreeRestart: fully restart tree and seeds at evenly spaced intervals.

Code stub for restart schedule

def tree_restart_schedule(T, mode="NoTreeRestart", checkpoints=None):
    if mode == "NoTreeRestart":
        return {t: tree_nodes_for_round(t) for t in range(1, T+1)}
    if mode == "SometimesRestart":
        if checkpoints is None: checkpoints = [int(T*0.33), int(T*0.66)]
        mapping = {}
        start = 1
        for cp in checkpoints + [T+1]:
            for t in range(start, cp):
                nodes = []
                k, x = 0, (t-start+1)
                while x > 0:
                    if x & 1: nodes.append((k, start + ((t-start+1) >> k) << k))
                    k += 1; x >>= 1
                mapping[t] = nodes
            start = cp
        return mapping
    if mode == "TreeRestart":
        period = max(1, int(np.sqrt(T)))
        mapping = {}
        for t in range(1, T+1):
            start_block = ((t-1)//period)*period + 1
            x = (t - start_block + 1)
            nodes = []
            k = 0
            while x > 0:
                if x & 1: nodes.append((k, start_block + ((t-start_block+1)>>k)<<k))
                k += 1; x >>= 1
            mapping[t] = nodes
        return mapping
    raise ValueError("Unknown mode")

Auditor procedures
- From ledger, reconstruct aggregate sketch-domain noise per round using auditor_reconstruct_noise; unsketch using published sketch parameters to obtain model-space noise; compute empirical covariance across repeated runs.
- Verify PSD and that covariance along each tree direction ≥ target (within tolerance). Also confirm number of rescue-noise activations matches ledger counts and that post-processed covariance meets target in rounds with L < L_min.
- Evaluate accuracy degradation vs NoTreeRestart in R1; ensure robustness gap ≤ 1.5 points in R2/R3.
- Fairness: enforce min-ON 0.2 with DP-throttled threshold adjustments; compute per-stratum ON rates (privately) and accuracy gaps.

Auditor core code skeleton (offline)

def auditor_verify_ledger(ledger_path):
    import json
    recs = [json.loads(l) for l in open(ledger_path)]
    ok = True
    for rec in recs:
        t = rec["round"]; m = rec["sketch_m"]
        seeds = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["vrf_seeds"].items()}
        vars_node = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["target_var_per_node"].items()}
        agg_noise = auditor_reconstruct_noise(t, m, seeds, vars_node, rec["L_min"], rec["realized_cohort"])
        # Compute empirical variance proxy
        var_emp = np.var(agg_noise)
        var_target_mean = np.mean(list(vars_node.values()))
        if var_emp + 1e-6 < 0.8 * var_target_mean:
            print(f"Round {t}: variance {var_emp} below target mean {var_target_mean}")
            ok = False
    return ok

Success metrics
- Audit passes: reconstructed covariances meet/exceed targets across rounds.
- Rescue noise triggers in expected fraction of rounds when realized cohort < L_min.
- Accuracy within 1.5 points across restart regimes; SometimesRestart appropriate for bursty participation.
- Fairness: worst-stratum ON rate ≥ 0.2 with ≤ 0.3ε extra budget; accuracy disparity not increased.

Ablation controls (all experiments)
- w/o residualization: set α=0, h_i frozen at 0; keep other modules.
- w/o DP gating: always ON; remove Laplace AboveThreshold; reallocate ε_gate to tree noise if holding ε fixed.
- w/o FO-aware compression: fix m to 1/8 of d; report misalignment metric.
- w/o DP quantiles: fixed clipping C_res; choose from small grid {median norm, 90th pct, 95th pct} non-DP.

Reliability and validity enhancements
- Multiple seeds and CIs; paired comparisons across seeds for higher power.
- Strict transparency ledger for public randomness seeds, sketch params, L_min, cohort sizes, and accountant budgets per round.
- Conservative accounting: approximate fixed-size sampling by Poisson q for RDP; validate with sensitivity analyses (vary q by ±10%).
- Audit script independent from training process; auditors need only ledger + model dimension + sketch code to verify noise guarantees.
- Sanity checks: compare aggregate DDG-TREE noise empirical distribution with discrete Gaussian (integer-rounded Gaussian) via QQ-plot; ensure no significant deviation.

Compute and runtime notes
- Simulation of 10k clients is memory-bound if naively storing per-client h_i; store h_i on disk or in memory-mapped arrays; or maintain h_i only for active clients plus a hash-based reservoir for inactive (approximation).
- Vectorize client residual computations when possible by batching synthetic clients with similar data sizes.
- Use mixed precision for local training to speed up; keep DP ops in float32.

Deliverables and plots
- Exp.1: accuracy vs rounds; final accuracy vs ε; residual norm trajectories; clipping utilization; SNR over rounds; log RMS noise vs log T slopes.
- Exp.2: accuracy vs bits curves; total uplink reduction; alignment ratio across rounds; ON/OFF rates and fairness (strata box plots).
- Exp.3: covariance verification heatmaps; rescue-noise counts; accuracy across restart regimes; fairness metrics.
- Release ledger and auditor script to reproduce noise verification.

What differs in baselines
- Baseline A (DP-FedAvg with i.i.d. Gaussian): set Gaussian std per round using dp_accounting to hit target ε under same q, T; no tree labels; no sketch alignment; optional QSGD/Top-k as in Exp.2.
- Baseline B (server DP-FTRL): emulate tree aggregation centrally; keep server-only noise; do not publish per-node seeds; no sketch alignment; same clipping.
- Baseline E (Adapt Norm): use its autotuned compression rule with independent Gaussian assumption; no residuals or gating; standard accountant.

Checklists per experiment
- Exp.1
  • Verify budget split and accountant ε ≤ target.
  • Record residual shrinkage and clipping utilization.
  • Train with 3 seeds per ε.
  • Fit noise growth slopes.
- Exp.2
  • Match ε across methods; record bits exactly.
  • Track alignment ratio per round.
  • Evaluate fairness of gating.
- Exp.3
  • Run R1–R3 with appropriate restart schedule.
  • Run auditor verification; publish results.
  • Measure rescue-noise activations.

Known limitations and mitigations
- Accounting approximation for tree-correlated noise via effective Gaussian steps may be conservative; report sensitivity across ±10% σ_eff.
- DP histogram for quantiles assumes bounded norms; we clip to a high percentile to bound sensitivity; verify chosen clip_max covers observed norms.
- SecAgg is simulated as summation; in production, integrate with an existing SecAgg library; our linear operations remain valid.

Summary of expected outcomes
- Exp.1: PACER-FL ≥ 2–5 point accuracy gains at ε∈[1,5] over i.i.d.-noise DP-FedAvg; clear residual shrinkage and √log T noise growth signature.
- Exp.2: ≥25% uplink saving at matched accuracy and privacy; compression error tracking DP noise; ON/OFF indistinguishable under SecAgg.
- Exp.3: Public audit verifies noise ≥ target under all dropout regimes; small accuracy deltas across restart schedules; fairness constraints effective within small extra ε.
experimental_design.experiment_code:
# PACER-FL Experiments: Auditable DDG-TREE + Residual DP Updates + DP Gating + FO-aware Compression
# Author: Creative AI Researcher
# Notes:
# - This script implements three experiments outlined in the specification.
# - It includes robust fallbacks if optional DP libraries are not available.
# - It uses PyTorch exclusively for deep learning; NumPy for array utilities.
# - All plots are saved as .pdf files suitable for inclusion in academic papers.
# - A quick test is provided to verify functionality in under a minute on CPU by using synthetic data and small configs.
# - For full experiments, increase dataset sizes, clients, and rounds per the specification.

import os
import json
import math
import time
import random
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")  # headless
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset, Subset

# Optional deps (fall back if unavailable)
try:
    from torchvision import datasets, transforms
    _HAS_TORCHVISION = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_TORCHVISION = False

try:
    from dp_accounting import rdp as rdp_mod
    _HAS_DP_ACCOUNTING = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_DP_ACCOUNTING = False

# diffprivlib optional
try:
    from diffprivlib.mechanisms import Laplace as DPLaplace
    from diffprivlib.tools import histogram as dp_histogram
    _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB = False

###############################################################################
# Utilities: RNG, printing required libraries
###############################################################################

REQUIRED_LIBRARIES = [
    "torch", "numpy", "matplotlib", "seaborn", "tqdm (optional)",
    "torchvision (recommended for CIFAR/EMNIST)",
    "dp-accounting (recommended)", "diffprivlib (recommended)", "pandas (optional)", "scipy (optional)", "pillow (for torchvision)"
]

def print_required_libraries():
    print("Required/Recommended Python libraries:")
    for lib in REQUIRED_LIBRARIES:
        print(" -", lib)


def set_seed(seed: int = 0):
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)

###############################################################################
# Data: Synthetic dataset for quick tests + optional CIFAR10/EMNIST loaders
###############################################################################

class SyntheticImageDataset(Dataset):
    """Small synthetic dataset for quick tests.
    Creates two classes of 8x8 or 16x16 RGB images with simple patterns.
    """
    def __init__(self, n_per_class=50, img_size=8, num_classes=10, seed=0):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.num_classes = num_classes
        self.images = []
        self.labels = []
        for c in range(num_classes):
            for _ in range(n_per_class):
                base = rng.random((3, img_size, img_size), dtype=np.float32)
                base += (c / max(num_classes-1, 1)) * 0.25
                base = np.clip(base, 0, 1)
                self.images.append(base.astype(np.float32))
                self.labels.append(c)
        self.images = np.stack(self.images)
        self.labels = np.array(self.labels, dtype=np.int64)
    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.labels)
    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        x = torch.tensor(self.images[idx])
        y = int(self.labels[idx])
        return x, y


def load_cifar10(data_root: str, seed: int = 0, limit_per_class: int = None):
    if not _HAS_TORCHVISION:
        raise RuntimeError("torchvision not available. Use synthetic dataset in quick test mode.")
    transform_train = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=4), transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    transform_test = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    trainset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=data_root, train=True, download=True, transform=transform_train)
    testset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=data_root, train=False, download=True, transform=transform_test)
    if limit_per_class is not None:
        # Reduce dataset size for faster runs
        train_indices = []
        counts = {c: 0 for c in range(10)}
        for i, y in enumerate(trainset.targets):
            if counts[y] < limit_per_class:
                train_indices.append(i)
                counts[y] += 1
        trainset = Subset(trainset, train_indices)
    return trainset, testset


###############################################################################
# Non-IID partitioning
###############################################################################

def dirichlet_noniid_split(labels: np.ndarray, num_clients: int, alpha: float = 0.1, min_size: int = 20, seed: int = 0):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    K = int(labels.max()) + 1
    idx_by_class = [np.where(labels == k)[0] for k in range(K)]
    for idx in idx_by_class:
        rng.shuffle(idx)
    proportions = rng.dirichlet(alpha=np.ones(num_clients), size=K)  # K x C
    client_indices = [[] for _ in range(num_clients)]
    for k in range(K):
        idx = idx_by_class[k]
        # Size allocated to each client proportional to the Dirichlet draw
        sizes = np.floor(proportions[k] / proportions[k].sum() * len(idx)).astype(int)
        # Adjust to ensure total matches
        while sizes.sum() < len(idx):
            sizes[rng.integers(0, num_clients)] += 1
        while sizes.sum() > len(idx):
            j = rng.integers(0, num_clients)
            if sizes[j] > 0:
                sizes[j] -= 1
        start = 0
        for c in range(num_clients):
            end = start + sizes[c]
            client_indices[c].extend(idx[start:end].tolist())
            start = end
    for c in range(num_clients):
        if len(client_indices[c]) < min_size:
            needed = min_size - len(client_indices[c])
            pool = np.setdiff1d(np.arange(len(labels)), np.array(client_indices[c], dtype=int), assume_unique=False)
            add = rng.choice(pool, size=min(needed, len(pool)), replace=False)
            client_indices[c].extend(add.tolist())
    return [np.array(sorted(list(set(idx))), dtype=int) for idx in client_indices]


def iid_split(num_samples: int, num_clients: int, min_size: int = 20, seed: int = 0):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    indices = np.arange(num_samples)
    rng.shuffle(indices)
    splits = np.array_split(indices, num_clients)
    res = []
    for arr in splits:
        if len(arr) < min_size:
            extra = rng.choice(indices, size=min_size - len(arr), replace=False)
            arr = np.concatenate([arr, extra])
        res.append(np.array(sorted(list(set(arr))), dtype=int))
    return res

###############################################################################
# Model definitions
###############################################################################

class SmallCIFAR10CNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
        self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv4 = nn.Conv2d(128, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(8*8*128, 256)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(256, num_classes)
    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.conv1(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv2(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = F.relu(self.conv3(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv4(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = torch.flatten(x, 1)
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return x


###############################################################################
# Flatten/unflatten helpers
###############################################################################

def flatten_params(model: nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    return torch.cat([p.detach().reshape(-1) for p in model.parameters()])


def assign_params(model: nn.Module, vec: torch.Tensor):
    offset = 0
    for p in model.parameters():
        num = p.numel()
        p.data.copy_(vec[offset:offset+num].reshape(p.shape))
        offset += num


###############################################################################
# CountSketch and FO-aware dimension selection
###############################################################################

class CountSketch:
    def __init__(self, d: int, m: int, seed: int = 0):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.h = rng.integers(0, m, size=d)
        self.s = rng.choice([-1, 1], size=d)
        self.m = m
        self.d = d
    def S(self, x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        y = np.zeros(self.m, dtype=np.float32)
        np.add.at(y, self.h, self.s * x)
        return y
    def unS(self, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        counts = np.bincount(self.h, minlength=self.m).astype(np.float32)
        inv = np.where(counts > 0, 1.0 / counts, 0.0)
        bucket_vals = y * inv
        return bucket_vals[self.h] * self.s


def choose_m_t(d: int, n_t: int, eps_eff_t: float, M_t: float, kappa: float = 1.2) -> int:
    m_est = int(kappa * min(d, max(8, (n_t * eps_eff_t * max(M_t, 1e-6))**2)))
    return int(max(16, min(d, m_est)))


def bits_per_client_countsketch(m: int, value_bits: int = 32, seed_bits: int = 64, extra_overhead_bits: int = 256) -> int:
    return m * value_bits + seed_bits + extra_overhead_bits


###############################################################################
# Tree schedule and DDG-TREE noise generation (client-side) + Auditor
###############################################################################

def tree_nodes_for_round(t: int):
    nodes = []
    k, x = 0, t
    while x > 0:
        if x & 1:
            nodes.append((k, (t >> k) << k))
        k += 1
        x >>= 1
    return nodes


def ddg_int_gaussian(shape, sigma, rng: np.random.Generator):
    return np.rint(rng.normal(0.0, sigma, size=shape)).astype(np.int64)


def client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t: int, sketch_dim: int, seeds: dict, target_var_per_node: dict, L_min: int, cohort_size: int):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    shares = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = math.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        scale = math.sqrt(max(L_min, cohort_size))
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma/scale, rngs[node])
        shares += z
    return shares


def auditor_reconstruct_noise(round_t: int, sketch_dim: int, seeds: dict, target_var_per_node: dict, L_min: int, realized_L: int):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    agg = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = math.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma, rngs[node])
        agg += z
    return agg


def tree_restart_schedule(T: int, mode: str = "NoTreeRestart", checkpoints=None):
    if mode == "NoTreeRestart":
        return {t: tree_nodes_for_round(t) for t in range(1, T+1)}
    if mode == "SometimesRestart":
        if checkpoints is None:
            checkpoints = [int(T*0.33), int(T*0.66)]
        mapping = {}
        start = 1
        for cp in checkpoints + [T+1]:
            for t in range(start, cp):
                nodes = []
                k, x = 0, (t-start+1)
                while x > 0:
                    if x & 1:
                        nodes.append((k, start + ((t-start+1) >> k) << k))
                    k += 1
                    x >>= 1
                mapping[t] = nodes
            start = cp
        return mapping
    if mode == "TreeRestart":
        period = max(1, int(math.sqrt(T)))
        mapping = {}
        for t in range(1, T+1):
            start_block = ((t-1)//period)*period + 1
            x = (t - start_block + 1)
            nodes = []
            k = 0
            while x > 0:
                if x & 1:
                    nodes.append((k, start_block + ((t-start_block+1)>>k)<<k))
                k += 1
                x >>= 1
            mapping[t] = nodes
        return mapping
    raise ValueError("Unknown mode")


###############################################################################
# DP mechanisms: DP gate (AboveThreshold) and DP quantile via DP histogram
###############################################################################

def laplace_noise(scale: float, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> float:
    if rng is None:
        rng = np.random.default_rng()
    u = rng.random() - 0.5
    return -scale * math.copysign(1.0, u) * math.log(1 - 2*abs(u) + 1e-12)


def dp_gate(score: float, theta: float, sensitivity: float, eps: float, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> bool:
    if _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB:
        mech = DPLaplace(epsilon=max(eps, 1e-8), sensitivity=max(sensitivity, 1e-12), random_state=rng)
        noisy_score = mech.randomise(score)
        return noisy_score >= theta
    else:
        b = sensitivity / max(eps, 1e-8)
        noisy_score = score + laplace_noise(b, rng=rng)
        return noisy_score >= theta


def private_quantile_from_histogram(norms: np.ndarray, q: float = 0.9, clip_max: float = 10.0, eps: float = 0.05, bins: int = 50, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> float:
    if rng is None:
        rng = np.random.default_rng()
    norms = np.clip(norms, 0.0, clip_max)
    if _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB:
        counts, edges = dp_histogram(norms, range=(0.0, clip_max), bins=bins, epsilon=max(eps, 1e-8), random_state=rng)
    else:
        counts, edges = np.histogram(norms, range=(0.0, clip_max), bins=bins)
        # Add Laplace noise to each bin count with sensitivity=1
        b = 1.0 / max(eps, 1e-8)
        noise = np.array([laplace_noise(b, rng=rng) for _ in range(len(counts))])
        counts = counts.astype(np.float64) + noise
        counts = np.clip(counts, 0.0, None)
    cdf = np.cumsum(counts) / max(np.sum(counts), 1e-9)
    idx = int(np.searchsorted(cdf, q))
    idx = min(max(idx, 0), len(edges) - 2)
    return float(edges[idx + 1])


###############################################################################
# Privacy accountant (RDP-based if available; otherwise simple placeholder)
###############################################################################

class Accountant:
    def __init__(self, delta: float = 1e-6):
        self.delta = delta
        self.events = []  # (type, payload)
    def add_gaussian_event(self, noise_multiplier: float, sampling_probability: float):
        self.events.append(("gauss", (noise_multiplier, sampling_probability)))
    def add_pure_eps(self, eps: float):
        self.events.append(("pure", eps))
    def get_epsilon(self) -> float:
        pure = sum(payload for typ, payload in self.events if typ == "pure")
        if _HAS_DP_ACCOUNTING:
            # Compose Gaussian RDP events
            orders = np.concatenate([np.linspace(1.25, 64, 200), np.array([128, 256])])
            total_rdp = np.zeros_like(orders, dtype=float)
            for typ, payload in self.events:
                if typ == "gauss":
                    noise_multiplier, q = payload
                    rdp = rdp_mod.compute_rdp(q=q, noise_multiplier=noise_multiplier, steps=1, orders=orders)
                    total_rdp += rdp
            if total_rdp.sum() > 0:
                eps_rdp, _, _ = rdp_mod.get_privacy_spent(orders=orders, rdp=total_rdp, delta=self.delta)
            else:
                eps_rdp = 0.0
            return float(eps_rdp + pure)
        else:
            # Fallback: naive composition (VERY conservative)
            gauss_eps = 0.0
            for typ, payload in self.events:
                if typ == "gauss":
                    noise_multiplier, q = payload
                    # approximate epsilon per step ~ q^2 / (2 * sigma^2), conservative
                    if noise_multiplier <= 0:
                        continue
                    gauss_eps += (q*q) / (2.0 * (noise_multiplier**2))
            return float(gauss_eps + pure)
    def reset(self):
        self.events = []


###############################################################################
# Federated client computation: residual update
###############################################################################

def client_compute_residual(model: nn.Module, data_loader: DataLoader, device: torch.device,
                            h_i_vec: np.ndarray, c_t_vec: np.ndarray, lr: float = 0.05, momentum: float = 0.9,
                            max_batches: int = 1) -> np.ndarray:
    local_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    local_model.load_state_dict(model.state_dict())
    opt = torch.optim.SGD(local_model.parameters(), lr=lr, momentum=momentum)
    local_model.train()
    batches = 0
    for xb, yb in data_loader:
        xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        opt.zero_grad()
        logits = local_model(xb)
        loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, yb)
        loss.backward()
        opt.step()
        batches += 1
        if batches >= max_batches:
            break
    g_i_vec = (flatten_params(local_model) - flatten_params(model)).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
    r_i = g_i_vec - h_i_vec
    return r_i


###############################################################################
# Evaluation utilities
###############################################################################

def evaluate_model(model: nn.Module, test_loader: DataLoader, device: torch.device) -> float:
    model.eval()
    correct, total = 0, 0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for xb, yb in test_loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
            logits = model(xb)
            preds = logits.argmax(dim=1)
            correct += int((preds == yb).sum().item())
            total += int(yb.numel())
    return 100.0 * correct / max(total, 1)


###############################################################################
# Transparency ledger
###############################################################################

def write_ledger_record(path: str, record_dict: dict):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True) if os.path.dirname(path) else None
    with open(path, "a") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(record_dict) + "\n")


def read_ledger(path: str):
    with open(path, "r") as f:
        return [json.loads(l) for l in f]


###############################################################################
# Experiment 1: Utility–privacy gains under non-IID
###############################################################################

def calibrate_sigma_for_budget(T: int, q: float, eps_target: float, delta: float, iters: int = 30) -> float:
    # Binary search sigma to match eps_target for T steps of Gaussian under sampling q
    acc = Accountant(delta=delta)
    def eps_for_sigma(sigma):
        acc.reset()
        for _ in range(T):
            acc.add_gaussian_event(noise_multiplier=sigma, sampling_probability=q)
        return acc.get_epsilon()
    lo, hi = 0.05, 100.0
    for _ in range(iters):
        mid = (lo + hi) / 2.0
        e = eps_for_sigma(mid)
        if e > eps_target:
            lo = mid
        else:
            hi = mid
    return hi


def experiment1_pacer_vs_baselines(config: dict):
    print("[Experiment 1] Starting: Utility–privacy under non-IID with PACER-FL vs baselines")
    set_seed(config.get("seed", 0))
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() and config.get("use_cuda", False) else "cpu")

    # Data selection
    use_synth = config.get("use_synthetic", True)
    num_classes = 10
    if use_synth:
        n_pc = config.get("synth_n_per_class", 30)
        img_size = config.get("synth_img_size", 8)
        train_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=n_pc, img_size=img_size, num_classes=num_classes, seed=config.get("seed", 0))
        test_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=max(10, n_pc//2), img_size=img_size, num_classes=num_classes, seed=config.get("seed", 1))
    else:
        train_ds, test_ds = load_cifar10(config.get("data_root", "./data"), seed=config.get("seed", 0), limit_per_class=config.get("limit_per_class"))

    train_labels = []
    for i in range(len(train_ds)):
        train_labels.append(int(train_ds[i][1]))
    train_labels = np.array(train_labels)

    N = config.get("N", 200)  # total clients
    T = config.get("T", 20)
    n_t = config.get("n_t", 20)
    q = n_t / N
    batch_size = config.get("batch_size", 32)
    alpha_dir = config.get("alpha_dir", 0.1)

    # Partition: non-IID and IID to show multiple patterns
    client_indices_non_iid = dirichlet_noniid_split(train_labels, num_clients=N, alpha=alpha_dir, min_size=max(5, batch_size), seed=config.get("seed", 0))
    client_indices_iid = iid_split(len(train_labels), num_clients=N, min_size=max(5, batch_size), seed=config.get("seed", 1))

    # Initialize model
    global_model_pacer = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    global_model_base = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)

    d = sum(p.numel() for p in global_model_pacer.parameters())

    # Accountant and budgets
    eps_total = config.get("eps", 2.0)
    delta = config.get("delta", 1e-6)
    eps_tree = 0.6 * eps_total
    eps_gate_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_clip_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_gate_round = eps_gate_total / T
    eps_clip_round = eps_clip_total / T

    # Calibrate sigma for Gaussian baseline (i.i.d noise)
    sigma_base = calibrate_sigma_for_budget(T=T, q=q, eps_target=eps_total, delta=delta)
    print(f"[Experiment 1] Calibrated baseline i.i.d. Gaussian sigma={sigma_base:.3f} for eps≈{eps_total}")

    # Calibrate sigma for PACER (effective per-round Gaussian event)
    sigma_eff_global = calibrate_sigma_for_budget(T=T, q=q, eps_target=eps_tree, delta=delta)
    print(f"[Experiment 1] Calibrated PACER tree-effective sigma={sigma_eff_global:.3f} for eps_tree≈{eps_tree:.3f}")

    # PACER state
    beta = config.get("beta", 0.1)
    alpha_cv = config.get("alpha_cv", 0.2)
    c_t_vec = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
    h_i = [np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32) for _ in range(N)]
    L_min = config.get("L_min", max(1, int(0.8 * n_t)))
    rng = np.random.default_rng(config.get("seed", 0))

    # VRF-like seeds and per-node targets
    vrf_seeds = {}
    target_var_per_node = {}
    for t in range(1, T+1):
        for node in tree_nodes_for_round(t):
            if node not in vrf_seeds:
                vrf_seeds[node] = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
            if node not in target_var_per_node:
                target_var_per_node[node] = 1.0  # scaled later

    ledger_path = config.get("ledger_path", "ledger_exp1.jsonl")
    if os.path.exists(ledger_path):
        os.remove(ledger_path)

    # Metrics logs
    accs_pacer, accs_base = [], []
    losses_pacer, losses_base = [], []
    on_rates = []
    residual_norms_mean = []
    sketches_m = []

    # Test loader
    test_loader = DataLoader(test_ds, batch_size=128, shuffle=False)

    # Initial sketch params
    eps_eff_round = eps_tree / T
    M_prev = 0.1

    # Baseline optimizer placeholders (server-side FedAvg with noise at server)
    # We simulate baseline as simple FedAvg + server Gaussian noise on aggregate.

    # Training rounds
    for t in range(1, T+1):
        # Use non-IID for odd t, IID for even t to show multiple data patterns
        client_indices = client_indices_non_iid if (t % 2 == 1) else client_indices_iid
        cohort = rng.choice(N, size=n_t, replace=False)

        # PACER: choose sketch dimension
        m_t = choose_m_t(d=d, n_t=n_t, eps_eff_t=eps_eff_round, M_t=M_prev, kappa=1.2)
        sketch_seed = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
        sketch = CountSketch(d=d, m=m_t, seed=sketch_seed)
        sketches_m.append(m_t)

        # Scale target variances globally for this run
        scale_factor = (sigma_eff_global**2) / max(1e-6, float(np.mean(list(target_var_per_node.values()))))
        target_var_scaled = {k: v*scale_factor for k, v in target_var_per_node.items()}

        # Compute per-client residual norms for quantile clipping
        per_client_norms = []
        # Pre-compute loader per chosen clients to avoid multiple passes
        client_loaders = {}
        for i in cohort:
            idxs = client_indices[i]
            client_loaders[i] = DataLoader(Subset(train_ds, idxs), batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model_pacer, client_loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            per_client_norms.append(np.linalg.norm(r_i))
        clip_max = float(np.percentile(per_client_norms, 99.0) + 1.0)
        C_res_t = private_quantile_from_histogram(np.array(per_client_norms, dtype=np.float32), q=0.9, clip_max=clip_max, eps=eps_clip_round, bins=30, rng=rng)

        # Public gate threshold schedule: simple heuristic by round
        theta_t = 0.0 if t < T/2 else 0.05

        # PACER uploads
        uploads = []
        ON_flags = []
        realized_ON = 0
        for i in cohort:
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model_pacer, client_loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(r_i) + 1e-12)
            scale = min(1.0, C_res_t / norm) if C_res_t > 0 else 1.0
            r_i = (r_i * scale).astype(np.float32)
            # score for gating
            cos_sim = float(np.dot(r_i, c_t_vec) / (np.linalg.norm(r_i)*np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)+1e-12)) if np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)>0 else 0.0
            s_i = max(cos_sim, 0.1 * norm / max(C_res_t, 1e-6))
            ON = dp_gate(s_i, theta_t, sensitivity=1.0, eps=eps_gate_round, rng=rng)
            ON_flags.append(ON)
            if not ON:
                r_i = np.zeros_like(r_i)
            y_i = sketch.S(r_i)
            shares_i = client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t=t, sketch_dim=m_t, seeds=vrf_seeds, target_var_per_node=target_var_scaled, L_min=L_min, cohort_size=n_t)
            uploads.append((y_i.astype(np.float32), shares_i))
            realized_ON += int(ON)
            # Update client control variate h_i with server c_t
            h_i[i] = (1.0 - alpha_cv) * h_i[i] + alpha_cv * c_t_vec

        # SecAgg aggregate in sketch space and unsketch
        Y_sum = np.sum([u[0] for u in uploads], axis=0)
        Shares_sum = np.sum([u[1] for u in uploads], axis=0).astype(np.float32)
        sketch_agg = Y_sum + Shares_sum
        agg_update = sketch.unS(sketch_agg)
        # Normalize by number of ON clients to get mean update (avoid zero)
        denom = max(realized_ON, 1)
        vec = flatten_params(global_model_pacer).cpu().numpy()
        vec_new = vec + agg_update / float(denom)
        assign_params(global_model_pacer, torch.tensor(vec_new, dtype=torch.float32))
        # Update c_t
        c_t_vec = (1.0 - beta) * c_t_vec + beta * (agg_update / float(denom))

        # Metrics PACER
        acc_pacer = evaluate_model(global_model_pacer, DataLoader(test_ds, batch_size=128, shuffle=False), device)
        accs_pacer.append(acc_pacer)
        on_rate = sum(ON_flags) / len(ON_flags)
        on_rates.append(on_rate)
        residual_norms_mean.append(float(np.mean(per_client_norms)))
        losses_pacer.append(float(np.mean(per_client_norms)))  # proxy

        # Baseline (i.i.d. Gaussian at server): compute mean of client updates, add Gaussian noise
        updates = []
        for i in cohort:
            # Simple FedAvg client update (one batch)
            local_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
            local_model.load_state_dict(global_model_base.state_dict())
            opt = torch.optim.SGD(local_model.parameters(), lr=0.05, momentum=0.9)
            for xb, yb in client_loaders[i]:
                xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
                opt.zero_grad()
                logits = local_model(xb)
                loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, yb)
                loss.backward()
                opt.step()
                break
            update_vec = (flatten_params(local_model) - flatten_params(global_model_base)).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
            updates.append(update_vec)
        if updates:
            mean_update = np.mean(np.stack(updates, axis=0), axis=0)
        else:
            mean_update = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
        # Add i.i.d. Gaussian noise (server-side noise after SecAgg)
        noise = np.random.normal(loc=0.0, scale=sigma_base, size=d).astype(np.float32)
        vec_b = flatten_params(global_model_base).cpu().numpy()
        vec_b_new = vec_b + mean_update + noise
        assign_params(global_model_base, torch.tensor(vec_b_new, dtype=torch.float32))
        acc_base = evaluate_model(global_model_base, DataLoader(test_ds, batch_size=128, shuffle=False), device)
        accs_base.append(acc_base)
        losses_base.append(float(np.mean([np.linalg.norm(u) for u in updates])) if updates else 0.0)

        # Ledger logging for auditor
        write_ledger_record(ledger_path, {
            "round": t,
            "vrf_seeds": {str(k): int(v) for k, v in vrf_seeds.items()},
            "target_var_per_node": {str(k): float(v) for k, v in target_var_scaled.items()},
            "L_min": int(L_min),
            "realized_cohort": int(n_t),
            "sketch_seed": int(sketch_seed),
            "sketch_m": int(m_t),
            "clip_quantiles": {"global": float(C_res_t)},
            "gate_theta": float(theta_t),
            "eps_eff_round": float(eps_eff_round),
            "eps_gate_round": float(eps_gate_round),
            "eps_clip_round": float(eps_clip_round),
            "restart_mode": "NoTreeRestart"
        })

        print(f"Round {t:03d}: PACER acc={acc_pacer:.2f}%, Base acc={acc_base:.2f}%, ON-rate={on_rate:.2f}, m_t={m_t}")

    # Plots for Experiment 1
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), accs_pacer, label="PACER-FL")
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), accs_base, label="DP-FedAvg (i.i.d.)")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Test Accuracy (%)")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Accuracy vs Rounds")
    plt.savefig("accuracy_pacerfl_vs_baselines.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), losses_pacer, label="PACER proxy loss (residual norm)")
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), losses_base, label="Baseline proxy loss")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Proxy Loss / Norm")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Training proxy loss vs Rounds")
    plt.savefig("training_loss_pacerfl_vs_baselines.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), residual_norms_mean, label="Mean residual norm")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Mean ||r_i||")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Residual norm trajectory")
    plt.savefig("residual_norms_pacerfl.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), on_rates, label="ON-rate")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("ON-rate")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("DP gating ON-rate")
    plt.savefig("on_rate_pacerfl.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    print("[Experiment 1] Completed. Figures saved: accuracy_pacerfl_vs_baselines.pdf, training_loss_pacerfl_vs_baselines.pdf, residual_norms_pacerfl.pdf, on_rate_pacerfl.pdf")

    return {
        "accs_pacer": accs_pacer,
        "accs_base": accs_base,
        "sketch_dims": sketches_m,
        "ledger_path": ledger_path
    }


###############################################################################
# Experiment 2: Communication–efficiency frontier and FO-alignment
###############################################################################

def estimate_dp_noise_mse(sigma_eff: float, d: int) -> float:
    return float(d) * (sigma_eff ** 2)


def estimate_sketch_mse(x: np.ndarray, sketch: CountSketch) -> float:
    y = sketch.S(x)
    x_hat = sketch.unS(y)
    return float(np.mean((x_hat - x)**2))


def experiment2_comm_efficiency(config: dict):
    print("[Experiment 2] Starting: Communication–efficiency with FO-aware compression + DP gating")
    set_seed(config.get("seed", 0))
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() and config.get("use_cuda", False) else "cpu")

    # Data (small synthetic for speed)
    use_synth = config.get("use_synthetic", True)
    num_classes = 10
    if use_synth:
        train_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=config.get("synth_n_per_class", 20), img_size=config.get("synth_img_size", 8), num_classes=num_classes, seed=config.get("seed", 0))
        test_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=config.get("synth_n_per_class", 10), img_size=config.get("synth_img_size", 8), num_classes=num_classes, seed=config.get("seed", 1))
    else:
        train_ds, test_ds = load_cifar10(config.get("data_root", "./data"), seed=config.get("seed", 0), limit_per_class=config.get("limit_per_class", 50))

    labels = []
    for i in range(len(train_ds)):
        labels.append(int(train_ds[i][1]))
    labels = np.array(labels)

    N = config.get("N", 100)
    T = config.get("T", 15)
    n_t = config.get("n_t", 10)
    q = n_t / N
    batch_size = config.get("batch_size", 32)

    client_indices = dirichlet_noniid_split(labels, num_clients=N, alpha=config.get("alpha_dir", 0.1), min_size=max(5, batch_size), seed=config.get("seed", 0))

    # Models
    model_pacer = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    d = sum(p.numel() for p in model_pacer.parameters())

    # PACER configs
    eps_total = config.get("eps", 2.0)
    delta = config.get("delta", 1e-6)
    eps_tree = 0.6 * eps_total
    eps_gate_round = (0.2 * eps_total) / T
    eps_clip_round = (0.2 * eps_total) / T
    sigma_eff_global = calibrate_sigma_for_budget(T=T, q=q, eps_target=eps_tree, delta=delta)

    c_t_vec = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
    h_i = [np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32) for _ in range(N)]

    rng = np.random.default_rng(config.get("seed", 0))

    # Seeds and variances
    vrf_seeds = {}
    target_var_per_node = {}
    for t in range(1, T+1):
        for node in tree_nodes_for_round(t):
            if node not in vrf_seeds:
                vrf_seeds[node] = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
            if node not in target_var_per_node:
                target_var_per_node[node] = 1.0
    scale_factor = (sigma_eff_global**2) / max(1e-6, float(np.mean(list(target_var_per_node.values()))))
    target_var_scaled = {k: v*scale_factor for k, v in target_var_per_node.items()}

    # Metrics
    bits_per_round_pacer = []
    bits_per_round_pacer_wo_gate = []
    accs_pacer = []
    align_ratios = []

    test_loader = DataLoader(test_ds, batch_size=128, shuffle=False)

    # Training few rounds
    eps_eff_round = eps_tree / T
    M_prev = 0.1
    L_min = max(1, int(0.8 * n_t))

    for t in range(1, T+1):
        cohort = rng.choice(N, size=n_t, replace=False)
        # FO-aware sketch
        m_t = choose_m_t(d=d, n_t=n_t, eps_eff_t=eps_eff_round, M_t=M_prev, kappa=1.2)
        sketch_seed = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
        sketch = CountSketch(d=d, m=m_t, seed=sketch_seed)

        # DP clipping threshold via DP histogram (global)
        norms = []
        loaders = {}
        for i in cohort:
            idxs = client_indices[i]
            loaders[i] = DataLoader(Subset(train_ds, idxs), batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(model_pacer, loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            norms.append(np.linalg.norm(r_i))
        C_res_t = private_quantile_from_histogram(np.array(norms, dtype=np.float32), q=0.9, clip_max=float(np.percentile(norms, 99.0) + 1.0), eps=eps_clip_round, bins=30, rng=rng)

        theta_t = 0.0 if t < T/2 else 0.05
        uploads_on = []
        uploads_always = []
        realized_ON = 0
        for i in cohort:
            r_i = client_compute_residual(model_pacer, loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(r_i) + 1e-12)
            scale = min(1.0, C_res_t / norm) if C_res_t > 0 else 1.0
            r_i = (r_i * scale).astype(np.float32)
            cos_sim = float(np.dot(r_i, c_t_vec) / (np.linalg.norm(r_i)*np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)+1e-12)) if np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)>0 else 0.0
            s_i = max(cos_sim, 0.1 * norm / max(C_res_t, 1e-6))
            ON = dp_gate(s_i, theta_t, sensitivity=1.0, eps=eps_gate_round, rng=rng)
            y_i = sketch.S(r_i if ON else np.zeros_like(r_i))
            shares_i = client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t=t, sketch_dim=m_t, seeds=vrf_seeds, target_var_per_node=target_var_scaled, L_min=L_min, cohort_size=n_t)
            uploads_on.append((y_i.astype(np.float32), shares_i))
            # without gating: always send r_i
            y_i_full = sketch.S(r_i)
            uploads_always.append((y_i_full.astype(np.float32), shares_i))
            realized_ON += int(ON)
            h_i[i] = 0.8 * h_i[i] + 0.2 * c_t_vec

        # Aggregate with gating
        Ysum = np.sum([u[0] for u in uploads_on], axis=0)
        Zsum = np.sum([u[1] for u in uploads_on], axis=0).astype(np.float32)
        agg = sketch.unS(Ysum + Zsum)
        denom = max(realized_ON, 1)
        vec = flatten_params(model_pacer).cpu().numpy()
        vec_new = vec + agg / float(denom)
        assign_params(model_pacer, torch.tensor(vec_new, dtype=torch.float32))
        c_t_vec = 0.9 * c_t_vec + 0.1 * (agg / float(denom))

        # Communication bits
        bits_pacer = bits_per_client_countsketch(m_t)
        bits_per_round_pacer.append(bits_pacer)
        bits_pacer_wo = bits_per_client_countsketch(m_t)
        bits_per_round_pacer_wo_gate.append(bits_pacer_wo)

        # Alignment ratio (sketch MSE / DP noise MSE)
        probe = np.random.normal(0, 1, size=d).astype(np.float32)
        mse_sketch = estimate_sketch_mse(probe, sketch)
        mse_dp = estimate_dp_noise_mse(sigma_eff_global, d)
        align = mse_sketch / max(mse_dp, 1e-9)
        align_ratios.append(float(align))

        acc = evaluate_model(model_pacer, test_loader, device)
        accs_pacer.append(acc)
        print(f"Round {t:03d}: bits/client={bits_pacer}, align={align:.3e}, acc={acc:.2f}%")

    # Plots
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), bits_per_round_pacer, label="PACER-FL (FO-aware)")
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), bits_per_round_pacer_wo_gate, label="PACER-wo-gate")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Bits per client")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Communication per round")
    plt.savefig("uplink_bits_pacerfl.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), align_ratios, label="Alignment ratio")
    plt.axhline(1.0, color='k', linestyle='--', linewidth=1)
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Sketch MSE / DP noise MSE")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("FO alignment ratio")
    plt.savefig("alignment_ratio_pacerfl.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), accs_pacer, label="PACER-FL acc")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy (%)")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Accuracy vs Rounds (PACER-FL)")
    plt.savefig("accuracy_pacerfl.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    print("[Experiment 2] Completed. Figures saved: uplink_bits_pacerfl.pdf, alignment_ratio_pacerfl.pdf, accuracy_pacerfl.pdf")
    return {
        "bits_pacer": bits_per_round_pacer,
        "align_ratios": align_ratios,
        "accs_pacer": accs_pacer
    }


###############################################################################
# Experiment 3: Auditability and robustness under dropouts and restarts
###############################################################################

def auditor_verify_ledger(ledger_path: str) -> bool:
    if not os.path.exists(ledger_path):
        print("Ledger not found:", ledger_path)
        return False
    recs = read_ledger(ledger_path)
    ok = True
    for rec in recs:
        t = rec["round"]; m = rec["sketch_m"]
        seeds = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["vrf_seeds"].items()}
        vars_node = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["target_var_per_node"].items()}
        agg_noise = auditor_reconstruct_noise(t, m, seeds, vars_node, rec["L_min"], rec["realized_cohort"]).astype(np.float32)
        var_emp = float(np.var(agg_noise))
        var_target_mean = float(np.mean(list(vars_node.values())))
        if var_emp + 1e-6 < 0.5 * var_target_mean:
            print(f"[Auditor] Round {t}: empirical noise variance {var_emp:.4f} below 0.5x target mean {var_target_mean:.4f}")
            ok = False
    return ok


def experiment3_auditability(config: dict):
    print("[Experiment 3] Starting: Auditability & robustness under dropouts and restarts")
    set_seed(config.get("seed", 0))
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() and config.get("use_cuda", False) else "cpu")

    # Use minimal synthetic data
    train_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=config.get("synth_n_per_class", 20), img_size=config.get("synth_img_size", 8), num_classes=10, seed=config.get("seed", 0))
    test_ds = SyntheticImageDataset(n_per_class=config.get("synth_n_per_class", 10), img_size=config.get("synth_img_size", 8), num_classes=10, seed=config.get("seed", 1))

    labels = np.array([int(train_ds[i][1]) for i in range(len(train_ds))])

    N = config.get("N", 80)
    n_t = config.get("n_t", 8)
    T = config.get("T", 12)
    q = n_t / N
    batch_size = config.get("batch_size", 32)

    client_indices = dirichlet_noniid_split(labels, num_clients=N, alpha=config.get("alpha_dir", 0.1), min_size=max(5, batch_size), seed=config.get("seed", 0))

    model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    d = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())

    eps_total = config.get("eps", 2.0)
    delta = config.get("delta", 1e-6)
    eps_tree = 0.6 * eps_total
    eps_gate_round = (0.2 * eps_total) / T
    eps_clip_round = (0.2 * eps_total) / T
    sigma_eff_global = calibrate_sigma_for_budget(T=T, q=q, eps_target=eps_tree, delta=delta)

    rng = np.random.default_rng(config.get("seed", 0))

    restart_mode = config.get("restart_mode", "SometimesRestart")
    sched = tree_restart_schedule(T, mode=restart_mode)

    # VRF seeds only for nodes in schedule
    vrf_seeds = {}
    target_var_per_node = {}
    for t in range(1, T+1):
        for node in sched[t]:
            if node not in vrf_seeds:
                vrf_seeds[node] = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
            if node not in target_var_per_node:
                target_var_per_node[node] = 1.0
    scale_factor = (sigma_eff_global**2) / max(1e-6, float(np.mean(list(target_var_per_node.values()))))
    target_var_scaled = {k: v*scale_factor for k, v in target_var_per_node.items()}

    # Ledger path
    ledger_path = config.get("ledger_path", f"ledger_exp3_{restart_mode}.jsonl")
    if os.path.exists(ledger_path):
        os.remove(ledger_path)

    # Dropout regimes
    regime = config.get("regime", "R2")  # R1 mild, R2 heavy, R3 bursty
    if regime == "R1":
        p_dropout = 0.2
    elif regime == "R2":
        p_dropout = 0.6
    else:  # R3 bursty
        p_dropout = None  # handled per round

    L_min = max(1, int(0.8 * n_t))
    c_t_vec = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
    h_i = [np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32) for _ in range(N)]

    # Metrics
    accs = []
    realized_cohorts = []

    for t in range(1, T+1):
        # Dropout pattern and cohort selection
        if regime == "R3":
            p = 0.2 if (t % 4 in [1,2]) else 0.8
        else:
            p = p_dropout
        # Sample base pool then apply dropout
        base = rng.choice(N, size=n_t, replace=False)
        mask = rng.random(size=n_t) > p
        cohort = base[mask]
        realized_L = len(cohort)
        realized_cohorts.append(realized_L)
        # If empty, resample once to avoid degenerate
        if realized_L == 0:
            cohort = rng.choice(N, size=max(1, n_t//2), replace=False)
            realized_L = len(cohort)

        # Sketch selection
        eps_eff_round = eps_tree / T
        m_t = choose_m_t(d=d, n_t=max(realized_L, 1), eps_eff_t=eps_eff_round, M_t=0.1, kappa=1.2)
        sketch_seed = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
        sketch = CountSketch(d=d, m=m_t, seed=sketch_seed)

        # Clipping
        norms = []
        loaders = {}
        for i in cohort:
            idxs = client_indices[i]
            loaders[i] = DataLoader(Subset(train_ds, idxs), batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(model, loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            norms.append(np.linalg.norm(r_i))
        C_res_t = private_quantile_from_histogram(np.array(norms, dtype=np.float32) if norms else np.array([0.0]), q=0.9, clip_max=float(np.percentile(norms, 99.0)+1.0) if norms else 1.0, eps=eps_clip_round, bins=30, rng=rng)

        # Uploads with DP gating (threshold schedule simple)
        theta_t = 0.0
        uploads = []
        realized_ON = 0
        for i in cohort:
            r_i = client_compute_residual(model, loaders[i], device, h_i[i], c_t_vec, max_batches=1)
            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(r_i) + 1e-12)
            scale = min(1.0, C_res_t / norm) if C_res_t > 0 else 1.0
            r_i = (r_i * scale).astype(np.float32)
            cos_sim = float(np.dot(r_i, c_t_vec) / (np.linalg.norm(r_i)*np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)+1e-12)) if np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)>0 else 0.0
            s_i = max(cos_sim, 0.1 * norm / max(C_res_t, 1e-6))
            ON = dp_gate(s_i, theta_t, sensitivity=1.0, eps=(0.2*eps_total)/T, rng=rng)
            y_i = sketch.S(r_i if ON else np.zeros_like(r_i))
            shares_i = client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t=t, sketch_dim=m_t, seeds=vrf_seeds, target_var_per_node=target_var_scaled, L_min=L_min, cohort_size=max(realized_L, 1))
            uploads.append((y_i.astype(np.float32), shares_i))
            realized_ON += int(ON)
            h_i[i] = 0.8 * h_i[i] + 0.2 * c_t_vec

        # Aggregate
        Ysum = np.sum([u[0] for u in uploads], axis=0) if uploads else np.zeros(m_t, dtype=np.float32)
        Zsum = np.sum([u[1] for u in uploads], axis=0).astype(np.float32) if uploads else np.zeros(m_t, dtype=np.float32)
        agg = sketch.unS(Ysum + Zsum)
        denom = max(realized_ON, 1)
        vec = flatten_params(model).cpu().numpy()
        vec_new = vec + agg / float(denom)
        assign_params(model, torch.tensor(vec_new, dtype=torch.float32))
        c_t_vec = 0.9 * c_t_vec + 0.1 * (agg / float(denom))

        # Ledger record
        write_ledger_record(ledger_path, {
            "round": t,
            "vrf_seeds": {str(k): int(v) for k, v in vrf_seeds.items()},
            "target_var_per_node": {str(k): float(target_var_scaled.get(k, 0.0)) for k in target_var_scaled},
            "L_min": int(L_min),
            "realized_cohort": int(realized_L),
            "sketch_seed": int(sketch_seed),
            "sketch_m": int(m_t),
            "clip_quantiles": {"global": float(C_res_t)},
            "gate_theta": float(theta_t),
            "eps_eff_round": float(eps_eff_round),
            "eps_gate_round": float((0.2*eps_total)/T),
            "eps_clip_round": float(eps_clip_round),
            "restart_mode": restart_mode
        })

        acc = evaluate_model(model, DataLoader(test_ds, batch_size=128, shuffle=False), device)
        accs.append(acc)
        print(f"Round {t:03d} [{regime}/{restart_mode}]: realized_L={realized_L}, acc={acc:.2f}%")

    # Auditor verification and plots
    ok = auditor_verify_ledger(ledger_path)
    print("[Experiment 3] Auditor verification:", "PASS" if ok else "FAIL")

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), realized_cohorts, label="Realized cohort size")
    plt.axhline(L_min, color='r', linestyle='--', label='L_min')
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Cohort size")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Cohort sizes and L_min")
    plt.savefig("cohort_sizes_robustness.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    plt.plot(range(1, T+1), accs, label="Accuracy")
    plt.xlabel("Round")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy (%)")
    plt.legend()
    plt.title("Accuracy under restarts/dropouts")
    plt.savefig("accuracy_robustness.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

    # Simple audit variance plot for last round
    recs = read_ledger(ledger_path)
    if recs:
        rec = recs[-1]
        m = rec["sketch_m"]
        seeds = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["vrf_seeds"].items()}
        vars_node = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["target_var_per_node"].items()}
        agg_noise = auditor_reconstruct_noise(rec["round"], m, seeds, vars_node, rec["L_min"], rec["realized_cohort"]).astype(np.float32)
        plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
        sns.histplot(agg_noise, bins=30, stat='density')
        plt.title("Auditor reconstructed noise (sketch domain)")
        plt.xlabel("Value")
        plt.ylabel("Density")
        plt.savefig("audit_noise_hist.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
        plt.close()

    print("[Experiment 3] Completed. Figures saved: cohort_sizes_robustness.pdf, accuracy_robustness.pdf, audit_noise_hist.pdf")
    return {"auditor_ok": ok, "ledger_path": ledger_path}


###############################################################################
# Quick functional test (fast, CPU-friendly)
###############################################################################

def run_quick_test():
    print_required_libraries()
    print("Running quick functional test with synthetic data and small configs...")

    cfg1 = {
        "seed": 1, "use_cuda": False, "use_synthetic": True,
        "synth_n_per_class": 10, "synth_img_size": 8,
        "N": 40, "T": 8, "n_t": 8, "batch_size": 16,
        "alpha_dir": 0.3, "eps": 1.5, "delta": 1e-6,
        "ledger_path": "ledger_quick_exp1.jsonl"
    }
    res1 = experiment1_pacer_vs_baselines(cfg1)

    cfg2 = {
        "seed": 2, "use_cuda": False, "use_synthetic": True,
        "synth_n_per_class": 10, "synth_img_size": 8,
        "N": 40, "T": 8, "n_t": 8, "batch_size": 16,
        "alpha_dir": 0.1, "eps": 1.5, "delta": 1e-6
    }
    res2 = experiment2_comm_efficiency(cfg2)

    cfg3 = {
        "seed": 3, "use_cuda": False, "synth_n_per_class": 10, "synth_img_size": 8,
        "N": 40, "T": 8, "n_t": 8, "batch_size": 16,
        "alpha_dir": 0.1, "eps": 1.5, "delta": 1e-6,
        "restart_mode": "SometimesRestart", "regime": "R3",
        "ledger_path": "ledger_quick_exp3.jsonl"
    }
    res3 = experiment3_auditability(cfg3)

    # Minimal assertions/prints to verify successful execution
    print("Quick test results summary:")
    print(" - Exp1: acc_end_pacer=%.2f, acc_end_base=%.2f, ledger=%s" % (res1["accs_pacer"][-1], res1["accs_base"][-1], res1["ledger_path"]))
    print(" - Exp2: last_bits=%d, last_align=%.3e, last_acc=%.2f" % (res2["bits_pacer"][-1], res2["align_ratios"][-1], res2["accs_pacer"][-1]))
    print(" - Exp3: auditor=", "PASS" if res3["auditor_ok"] else "FAIL", ", ledger=", res3["ledger_path"]) 
    print("All figures saved as .pdf in current directory.")


###############################################################################
# Entry point
###############################################################################

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # By default, run the quick test. For full experiments, construct configs per the specification and call
    # experiment1_pacer_vs_baselines, experiment2_comm_efficiency, and experiment3_auditability.
    run_quick_test()
